# Gameplay instructions

- Treat the project as post-M30. `docs/content_scope_v2.md` is complete and ready to archive.
- Current gameplay foundations include deterministic scenario outcomes, Scenario Result presentation, Scenario `playerStart`, owned-service economy, Trading Post interaction, guarded and unguarded player-side service claiming, v1 strategic-economy proof content, player-facing mine stationing/unstationing, a bounded read-only owned-service overview / strategic service readout panel, a bounded unit-storage foundation, cross-Region generic-unit travel loss with explicit warning/confirmation, and the M30 contested-infrastructure loop (service defense, storage loss with Temporarily Unavailable heroes, enemy capture pressure, destruction/restoration, service event log).
- The next selected milestone is not yet chosen. Use `docs/implementation_roadmap.md` as the source of truth.
- M29 travel loss is implemented narrowly: the at-risk set is exactly slotted active/reserve generic stacks. Stored and stationed stacks survive Region travel. Heroes and the Player Character travel. The warning is two-stage on the World Map screen; `TravelToRegion` owns the confirmed mutation.
- M28 storage is a DISTINCT placement bucket from M25 mine stationing: storage cap 7, units persist/retrieve, and "garrison" maps to M25 stationed guards rather than a separate implemented system. Store/retrieve owned non-PC stacks at a player-owned `Storage` service behind `GameSession` methods, preserving one-place-at-a-time. Additive `stored_units` save, no schema bump. `LocationServiceKind::Storage` is not `IsOwnableServiceKind`; storage claimability is deferred.
- M30 service defense: node-level attacks against player-owned attackable services (mine/trader/storage kinds; never Rest/Shop/Recruit/Muster; never the arrival node) resolve through `GameSession::ResolveServiceAttack` — pure deterministic `ServiceDefenseRules` strength comparison when the player is absent (defender wins ties), the existing interactive battle surface when the player stands on the node (`ApplyServiceDefenseVictory`/`Defeat`). Capture transfers ownership immediately and resolves placed stacks atomically: generics dismissed, heroes Temporarily Unavailable (weekly return-to-reserve at day start when a slot is free), Player Character never placed/lost/TU, refs cleared in the same mutation.
- M30 enemy pressure is bounded: `ProcessEnemyPhase` attack action (same node or adjacent, patrol-radius legal, current region only, deterministic target order, authored `enemyGroupId` strength). Enemy teams capture player-owned services only; enemy-side destruction/sabotage and enemy-vs-enemy contention are v3.
- M30 destruction/restoration is opt-in via authored `destroyable` + validated `restore_cost`: destroy = standing on the unoccupied node, no placed units, 1000 Energy + 1 hour; restore = authored resources, queues, completes at next day start before payout; destroy-again cancels the queue. Mine payout semantics are unchanged.
- Do NOT replace the deterministic defense resolver with a second battle engine; full-simulation auto-resolve/battle AI is a v3 candidate.
- M27 owned-service overview is READ-ONLY presentation (transient `OwnedServiceOverviewMode`, opened with `O` from Region mode): a pure mapper/render-model/renderer over existing `GameSession` accessors. It must not mutate ownership, stationing, storage, or payout. Do not add remote stationing, Storage/Garrison management, service repair/destruction, ownership transfer, or other service-management actions to it unless a scoped roadmap milestone explicitly selects that work.
- Ownership and guarding are related but separate: an owned service does not have to be guarded, and a guarded service is only one capture path. Claiming is systemic: legally entering an unguarded node claims its eligible ownable services via `GameSession::ResolveNodeEntryClaims`; guarded battle-before-placement is preserved.
- `mine_production` is player-facing: M25 added a bounded stationing flow at player-owned mines. Stationing is guard/worker capacity only — not Storage/Garrison.
- Do not add AI economy, full-simulation defense battles, shared hero pool, enemy-side destruction/sabotage, Market/Black Market/Freelancer behavior, or broader v3 systems without an explicit roadmap milestone.
- Gameplay-rule changes must be tested in pure/unit tests where practical and integration/end-to-end tests where the rule crosses systems.
